# frimpomaasync.com

> Done-for-you back-office automation for one-person businesses, built and run by NaNa Frimpomaa. Flagship offer: the Client Catcher — missed calls caught, leads followed up in the owner's voice, appointments booked, hands-off. Built for real estate agents and home-services owners (HVAC, plumbing) who answer their own phone.

## The offer: the Client Catcher

- What it does: catches missed calls, texts back within minutes in the owner's voice, qualifies the lead, books the appointment onto the owner's real calendar, chases quotes and no-shows, requests reviews, and keeps the CRM/calendar/spreadsheet updated.
- Price: $1,500 one-time build + optional $99/month care plan (monitoring, fixes, improvements). No lock-in — the care plan is optional and cancellable anytime.
- Founding rate: $750 for the first 3 clients, in exchange for a named testimonial once the system is live and booking (posted on the site and as a review on the frimpomaasync Facebook page). Founding clients keep that rate context forever; includes first 30 days of the care plan free.
- Time cost to the owner: one free 15-minute mapping call, then ~30 minutes reviewing the finished system. Done-for-you by design.
- Details: https://frimpomaasync.com/client-catcher.html

## Guarantee (defined terms, written into the client agreement)

"Live and working in 7 days, booking appointments within 30 — or you don't pay."

- "Live" = catching missed calls and sending follow-ups on the client's real phone number and calendar, verified together on a screen-share.
- "Booking appointments" = real prospective customers landing on the client's calendar. Test bookings do not count.
- The client's only obligation is running their business as usual — the guarantee does not require buying ads.
- "You don't pay" = full refund of the build fee, or cancellation of any unpaid balance. Not a credit.

## Ownership and hosting model

The system runs on the provider's software accounts so the client never buys software, manages subscriptions, or learns tools. The client owns everything built for them: workflows, scripts and messages in their voice, setup documentation, and all customer data. The agreement includes full handoff terms — data export, system documentation, and a transfer path — so the client's business never depends on the provider being reachable.

## Customer data

Leads' names, numbers, and messages move only between the client's phone, calendar, and the follow-up system. Nothing is sold or shared. Every provider that touches the data is named in the client agreement. On cancellation, data is exported to the client and deleted from the provider's accounts.

## Proof status (honest)

This is an early-stage offer: no client testimonials yet, and the site says so plainly. Instead of borrowed reviews it offers: (1) a live demo run on the prospect's own real leads before any payment, (2) working systems built in public across the site, (3) the provider runs the same system on her own business, and (4) the money-back guarantee above.

## Notes — free, ungated answer pages

Written by NaNa Frimpomaa for one-person businesses. Each one hands over the usable material (scripts, tables, real numbers) rather than gating it. Index: https://frimpomaasync.com/blog/

- How to reply to every lead in five minutes when you're the only one there — splits a reply into an instant automatic acknowledgement and a same-day human answer; five copy-paste texts; a four-touch follow-up ladder over seven days; free phone-only setup steps. https://frimpomaasync.com/blog/reply-to-every-lead-in-five-minutes
- What automating a one-person business actually costs in 2026 — five routes compared with real ranges (free phone setup, all-in-one software $97–$497/mo plus 10–20 setup hours, app patchwork, live answering service $250–$600/mo, virtual assistant $800–$1,600/mo, done-for-you build $800–$3,000 one time); hidden costs; a break-even formula. https://frimpomaasync.com/blog/what-automating-a-one-person-business-actually-costs
- Answering service vs AI receptionist vs missed-call text-back — capability comparison across five options, a decision tree based on where inquiries are lost, six questions to ask any vendor, and a mystery-call test. States plainly what a follow-up system cannot do. https://frimpomaasync.com/blog/answering-service-vs-ai-receptionist-vs-text-back
- How to stop no-shows without being the bad guy — a three-reminder cadence (instant confirmation, 24-hour reminder requesting a one-word reply, two-hour nudge), six copy-paste messages, deposit guidance, and cancellation-policy wording. https://frimpomaasync.com/blog/stop-no-shows-without-being-the-bad-guy
- Do I need a CRM if I'm a one-person business — answer: usually not at first. A nine-column lead sheet, four statuses (new, quoted, booked, closed), a ten-minute weekly review, and the five triggers for upgrading. https://frimpomaasync.com/blog/do-i-need-a-crm-if-im-a-one-person-business
- Your website doesn't have to be Squarespace or Shopify — why a one-person business often doesn't need a website platform. https://frimpomaasync.com/blog/your-website-doesnt-have-to-be-squarespace
